claims.claim: NLP scoring functions decompose into WFA over semirings
claims.section: §3 System Architecture, §4 The EvalSpec DSL (§4.4 Built-in Metrics)
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/scoring/*.rs — TripleMetric trait in mod.rs with 7 implementations: ExactMatchScorer, NormalizedExactMatchScorer (exact_match.rs), BleuScorer (bleu.rs), RougeNScorer, RougeLScorer (rouge.rs), TokenF1Scorer (token_f1.rs), RegexMatchScorer (regex_match.rs). Each implements score_reference(), score_automaton() (WFA-based), and score_circuit() (field arithmetic).
claims.notes: TripleMetric trait (mod.rs lines 34-64) enforces that every metric provides all three representations. score_and_verify() cross-checks all three agree.

claims.claim: Two-tier compilation: algebraic for embeddable, gadget-assisted for tropical
claims.section: §5 Two-Tier Compilation (§5.1 Tier 1: Algebraic, §5.2 Tier 2: Gadget-Assisted)
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/circuit/compiler.rs — compile_algebraic() (lines 1351-1467) for Tier 1, compile_gadget_assisted() (lines 1608-1801) for Tier 2, compile_hybrid() (lines 1805-1811) for automatic selection. Paper Theorem 1 (thm:tier1) proves Tier 1 soundness; Theorem 2 (thm:tier2) proves Tier 2 soundness.
claims.notes: Tier 1 handles F_p-embeddable semirings (counting, boolean) via direct homomorphism. Tier 2 uses comparison/range-check gadgets for tropical min-plus operations. Hybrid mode auto-selects based on semiring properties.

claims.claim: 118K lines of Rust
claims.evidence_location: wc -l of all .rs files in implementation/ yields ~155K lines; including .toml and .md brings total to ~161K. Paper no longer cites a specific LoC count in the abstract or contributions; Table 5 reports module-level breakdown.
claims.notes: The explicit '118K lines' claim was removed from the abstract and contributions list in the paper revision. Table 5 retains the per-module breakdown which sums to ~118K (likely non-blank, non-comment lines or excluding test files).

claims.claim: Differential testing over 100K random pairs with 0 disagreements
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/scoring/differential.rs — random_test_pairs() generates random pairs; assert!(report.is_perfect()) at lines 527-563 checks 0 disagreements per metric. Paper Table 3 reports 100K pairs × 8 metrics = 800K total, all with 0 discrepancies. CLI: `spectacles differential-test --count 100000 --seed 42`.
claims.notes: In-repo test uses smaller counts (50-100 pairs) for fast CI. The 100K count is the default for the CLI harness. All five core metrics assert is_perfect() (0 disagreements).

claims.claim: PSI protocol correctly computes intersection cardinality
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/psi/protocol.rs — PSIProtocol with run_local() and run_trie_based(). PSIResult contains intersection_cardinality field. 90+ test functions including: test_run_local_identical_sets(), test_run_local_disjoint_sets(), test_run_local_partial_overlap(), test_run_local_cardinality_only_mode(), test_run_trie_based_identical(), test_run_trie_based_disjoint(), test_run_trie_based_partial().
claims.notes: Tests cover identical, disjoint, and partial-overlap scenarios for both local and trie-based modes. PSIMode::CardinalityOnly reveals only |A ∩ B|. ContaminationAttestation and compute_contamination() provide downstream verification.

claims.claim: Semiring axioms satisfied by all implementations
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/tests/semiring_properties.rs — 37 proptest property tests (8 core axioms × 3 embeddable semirings = 24, plus 8 tropical axioms, plus 5 semiring-specific tests). Axioms: additive commutativity, additive associativity, additive identity, multiplicative associativity, multiplicative identity, zero annihilation, left distributivity, right distributivity.
claims.notes: Paper claims 38 properties; actual count is 37 test functions (31 unique property names expanded via macro across BooleanSemiring, CountingSemiring, GoldilocksField, TropicalSemiring). Near-exact match; minor discrepancy likely due to counting methodology.

claims.claim: EvalSpec has formal BNF grammar and typing rules
claims.section: §4 The EvalSpec DSL (§4.2 Formal Grammar, §4.3 Type System and Semiring Inference)
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/EVALSPEC_FORMAL.md — BNF grammar (Section 1) defining program/declaration/expression/type productions, typing rules (Section 2) with judgment Γ ⊢ e : τ. spectacles-core/src/evalspec/parser.rs (recursive descent with Pratt parsing, ~107KB), spectacles-core/src/evalspec/typechecker.rs (~1300 lines, bidirectional type checker with TypeChecker struct, TypeEnv, semiring lattice), spectacles-core/src/evalspec/types.rs (EvalType enum with Base/Semiring/Function/Metric variants).
claims.notes: Grammar covers declarations (metric, let, type, import, test), expressions with binding powers 1-7, and semiring types (Boolean, Counting, Tropical, BoundedCounting(k), Real, LogDomain, Viterbi, Goldilocks). Semiring subtyping lattice enforces compatibility.

claims.claim: Semi-honest PSI suffices under commit-then-execute
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/psi/protocol.rs — CommitmentBinding struct (lines 3574-3585) with commit(), verify(), commit_ngram_set(), verify_ngram_set(). InputVerifier struct with pre_protocol_check() for temporal + commitment validation. Tests: test_commitment_binding_roundtrip(), test_commitment_binding_rejects_different_set(), test_commitment_binding_rejects_subset(), test_commitment_binding_rejects_superset(), test_commitment_binding_order_independent(), test_input_verifier_pre_protocol_check_valid(), test_input_verifier_pre_protocol_check_temporal_violation().
claims.notes: Paper (lines 726-738) argues semi-honest PSI suffices because: (1) CommitmentBinding prevents input substitution, (2) completion is required, (3) OPRF deviation is detectable. Blake3-based commitment with nonce and timestamp.

claims.claim: Goldilocks field arithmetic is correct
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/circuit/goldilocks.rs — Goldilocks field (p = 2^64 - 2^32 + 1) implementation with Montgomery multiplication. spectacles-core/MATH_VERIFICATION.md documents 2 bugs found and fixed: (1) Montgomery inverse constant corrected from 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xFFFFFFFEFFFFFFFF, (2) Lagrange interpolation constant-term computation fixed. Both verified by property-based tests in semiring_properties.rs.
claims.notes: MATH_VERIFICATION.md provides detailed derivation of correct constant via -p^{-1} mod 2^64 and explains impact on montgomery_mul, montgomery_reduce, to_montgomery, from_montgomery paths.

claims.claim: Metric equivalence is decidable via Hopcroft minimization
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/wfa/equivalence.rs — check_via_minimization() (line 1082) and check_via_hopcroft_karp() (line 893). spectacles-core/src/wfa/minimization.rs — minimize_hopcroft() (line 498) implementing Hopcroft partition refinement with initial_partition_by_final_weight(), generic_partition_refinement(), weight_signature(). MinimizationStrategy enum: Hopcroft, MyhillNerode, Bisimulation, WeightedPartition, Hybrid.
claims.notes: Decidability applies to Boolean/tropical semirings via minimization to canonical forms + isomorphism check. For general semirings, equivalence is heuristic (noted at lines 2039-2041). Hopcroft-Karp variant uses union-find on product space with distinguishing-word generation.

claims.claim: Circuit complexity: trace width 2|Q|+O(1)
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/circuit/compiler.rs — compute_trace_layout() (lines 1898-1917): num_states state columns + num_states matmul_aux columns + 1 input_symbol + |Σ| selectors + 1 output + 1 step_counter = 2·|Q| + |Σ| + 3. Paper states '2n+O(1) columns' at line 540. Table 4 confirms: Exact Match (33 states → 68 columns ≈ 2×33+2), Token F1 (256 states → 514 columns ≈ 2×256+2).
claims.notes: The O(1) term absorbs the alphabet-size selectors (|Σ|) and 3 bookkeeping columns. For fixed alphabet, this is indeed 2|Q|+O(1). Table 4 data is consistent with the formula.

claims.claim: Lean-Rust correspondence verified
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/LEAN_RUST_CORRESPONDENCE.md — Documents three-layer verification: (1) Lean 4 machine-checked proofs of KleeneSemiring axioms, circuit soundness (Theorems 1 & 2), EvalSpec-to-WFA semantic preservation, WFA equivalence, semiring embeddings; (2) Property-based testing via proptest (semiring_properties.rs); (3) Differential testing on 100K input pairs. All major components show ✅ except Hopcroft minimization (⚠️ differentially tested, Lean proof pending).
claims.notes: The document describes the correspondence but the actual Lean proofs are referenced (not included in this repo). Hopcroft minimization Lean proof is explicitly marked as pending/deferred. Verification relies on the three-layer methodology rather than full machine-checked coverage.

claims.claim: End-to-end pipeline works with MMLU, SQuAD, and translation benchmarks
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-integration/tests/e2e_tests.rs — mod real_benchmark_tests (lines 427-722) containing: test_mmlu_style_benchmark_pipeline() (lines 449-526, ExactMatchScorer), test_squad_style_token_f1_pipeline() (lines 532-617, TokenF1Scorer), test_translation_bleu_pipeline() (lines 623-721, BleuScorer). Each test exercises full pipeline: scoring → triple verification → n-gram fingerprinting → commitment binding → PSI protocol → attestation → certificate generation.
claims.notes: Tests use representative benchmark-style data (not full benchmark datasets). MMLU uses multiple-choice exact match, SQuAD uses extractive QA token F1, translation uses corpus-level BLEU. All three exercise the complete end-to-end pipeline including contamination detection.

claims.claim: Montgomery inverse constant corrected
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/circuit/goldilocks.rs, spectacles-core/MATH_VERIFICATION.md (lines 196-206) — Incorrect value 0xFFFFFFFF corrected to 0xFFFFFFFEFFFFFFFF. Derivation: -p^{-1} mod 2^64 where p^{-1} mod 2^64 = 0x100000001. Impact: Montgomery multiplication path (montgomery_mul, montgomery_reduce, to_montgomery, from_montgomery) would produce incorrect results with old constant.
claims.notes: Bug was found during mathematical verification process. The constant is used in the Montgomery reduction step of Goldilocks field arithmetic. Fix verified by property-based test suite.

claims.claim: Lagrange interpolation bug fixed
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/circuit/goldilocks.rs, spectacles-core/MATH_VERIFICATION.md (lines 213-241) — Dead-code line corrupted basis[0] during basis polynomial multiplication by (x - x_j). Wrong: basis[0] = basis[0].neg_elem().mul_elem(xs[j]).neg_elem() (= basis[0] * xs[j]). Correct: basis[0] = Self::ZERO.sub_elem(basis[0].mul_elem(xs[j])). Verification: buggy code produced 225 instead of correct 15 for (x-3)(x-5).
claims.notes: Lagrange interpolation is used in FRI protocol and polynomial commitment verification. Bug would cause incorrect polynomial evaluations. Fix verified by both targeted test case and property-based suite. Paper notes these corrections do not affect differential testing results (line 1001) which use the standard reduction path.

claims.claim: Independent Python verification of semiring axioms
claims.evidence_location: implementation/tests/test_semiring_axioms.py — 22 test functions covering 8 core semiring axioms × 4 types, Kleene star unfolding, semiring embeddings, WFA equivalence brute-force enumeration, Montgomery constant verification, pass@k scaling characterization.
claims.notes: All 22 tests pass with 500 random trials per axiom. Provides cross-language (Python vs Rust) validation. Independently re-derives Montgomery inverse constant 0xFFFFFFFEFFFFFFFF.

claims.claim: PSI security under commit-then-execute
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/PSI_SECURITY_ANALYSIS.md — Formal analysis of three attack vectors (input substitution, selective abort, OPRF deviation) with defenses and residual limitations. spectacles-core/src/psi/protocol.rs — CommitmentBinding implementation with 7 test functions.
claims.notes: Commitment binding prevents input substitution (collision resistance). Completion requirement prevents selective abort. OPRF deviation partially addressed (spot-check; VOPRF not implemented). Limitations honestly documented: no UC security, no VOPRF, collusion out of scope.

claims.claim: Boolean embedding valid for deterministic WFA execution
claims.evidence_location: Paper Appendix B — Proof that ∨-to-+ mismatch never arises in deterministic WFAs. implementation/tests/test_semiring_axioms.py::test_boolean_embedding_into_goldilocks documents the caveat.
claims.notes: The Boolean ⊕ = ∨ is idempotent but field + is not. The embedding is valid because deterministic WFAs have at most one active transition per state, avoiding the case where 1 ∨ 1 ≠ 1 + 1.

claims.claim: Pass@k arithmetization scales as O(kt)
claims.evidence_location: Paper Table 6 (Pass@k scaling) and implementation/tests/test_semiring_axioms.py::test_pass_at_k_scaling. For k=100, t=100: 10K states, 20002 columns — feasible. For k=100, t=1000: 200K columns — approaches STARK limits.
claims.notes: Scaling is polynomial in k and t. Paper honestly states that large instances (k=100, t=1000) approach current STARK capacity limits.

claims.claim: EvalSpec has formal BNF grammar, typing rules, and denotational semantics
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/EVALSPEC_FORMAL.md — Full BNF grammar (§1, ~220 lines), typing rules as inference rules (§2, ~200 lines), denotational semantics mapping to formal power series (§3, ~180 lines), WFA-expressible fragment characterization with decidability theorem (§4).
claims.notes: Includes Theorem 4.1 (WFA expressibility ↔ rational formal power series) and Theorem 4.2 (equivalence decidability for commutative ring semirings). Expressibility boundary table (§4.3) honestly marks geometric mean, corpus BLEU, BERTScore, and calibration error as NOT WFA-expressible.
